evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my .NET for Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete SparkScan integration code into EmptyActivity.cs (new SparkScan(settings), SparkScanView.Create(...) with a SparkScanCoordinatorLayout parent, ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate, BarcodeScanned event or ISparkScanListener, sparkScanView.OnPause/OnResume lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, AndroidManifest permission, runtime permission, MainApplication initialize for SDK 8.0+, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding android.permission.CAMERA to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting the CAMERA permission at runtime
Setup checklist mentions creating a MainApplication subclass that calls ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() (required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions adding a SparkScanCoordinatorLayout element to the activity layout XML
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new SparkScanSettings() is used (not SparkScanSettings.Create(), which does not exist)
new SparkScan(settings) is used (not SparkScan.Create(...), which does not exist)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
FindViewById<SparkScanCoordinatorLayout>( is used to fetch the container view from the activity layout
SparkScanView.Create( is called with a SparkScanCoordinatorLayout (not an arbitrary ViewGroup) as the first argument
SparkScanView.Create( receives the DataCaptureContext as the context argument
SparkScanView.Create( receives the SparkScan instance as the sparkScan argument
SparkScanView.Create( receives a SparkScanViewSettings as the settings argument
sparkScan.BarcodeScanned += handler is wired (or sparkScan.AddListener if using ISparkScanListener)
The activity's OnPause forwards to sparkScanView.OnPause()
The activity's OnResume forwards to sparkScanView.OnResume()
No call to BarcodeCapture.Create or barcodeCapture.AddListener appears (SparkScan is not the same API as BarcodeCapture)
No DataCaptureView is created (SparkScan has its own pre-built UI and does not use DataCaptureView)
No BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created
No Camera.GetDefaultCamera() or dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(...) call is present (SparkScan manages its own camera internally)

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan to my existing .NET Android Activity. Here's the Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, log the barcode data.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates SparkScan into EmptyActivity.cs with QR and Data Matrix enabled. OnBarcodeScanned (or the BarcodeScanned event handler) logs the barcode data via Android.Util.Log or similar. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
new SparkScan( is used (not SparkScan.Create)
The BarcodeScanned event handler (or OnBarcodeScanned) reads args.Session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode or session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
barcode.Data is accessed
The scan result is logged (Log.Debug, System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine, Console.WriteLine, or similar)
sparkScanView.OnPause() and sparkScanView.OnResume() are wired
Setup checklist mentions Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages

evals.prompt: In my .NET Android SparkScan app, I want to reject barcodes whose value starts with '000' and show an error message in the SparkScan UI for 5 seconds before scanning resumes. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate, builds a SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback and a SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback(message, resumeCapturingDelay), assigns the delegate to sparkScanView.Feedback, and returns the error feedback when barcode.Data starts with '000'.

assertions.text: The activity (or a dedicated class) implements ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate
GetFeedbackForBarcode(Barcode barcode) is implemented
new SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( is constructed with a message argument and a resumeCapturingDelay argument
resumeCapturingDelay uses TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5) (or TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(5000)) — not a bare integer 5 or a TimeInterval
new SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() (or one of its multi-argument constructors) is used for the success case
sparkScanView.Feedback is assigned to the delegate instance (typically this)
GetFeedbackForBarcode inspects barcode.Data and returns the error feedback when it starts with '000'
GetFeedbackForBarcode does NOT call RunOnUiThread, Handler.Post, or any UI dispatcher (the delegate runs on a background thread and the feedback objects must already be cached)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, SparkScanCoordinatorLayout, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: Hide the torch and trigger buttons on my .NET Android SparkScanView and add a custom Start button that calls StartScanning() programmatically.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets sparkScanView.TorchControlVisible = false and sparkScanView.TriggerButtonVisible = false, then wires a button click handler that calls sparkScanView.StartScanning(). Uses the renamed v7 property TorchControlVisible (not the v6 TorchButtonVisible).

assertions.text: sparkScanView.TorchControlVisible = false is set (not TorchButtonVisible, which was removed in v7)
sparkScanView.TriggerButtonVisible = false is set
sparkScanView.StartScanning() is called from a click handler or button event
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored in my .NET Android SparkScan app.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the SparkScanSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, not TimeInterval).

assertions.text: settings.CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not TimeInterval.Millis, not a CodeDuplicate.DefaultDuplicateFilter sentinel)
The value is applied to a SparkScanSettings instance (not BarcodeCaptureSettings)
Either CodeDuplicateFilter is set before new SparkScan(settings) is called, or sparkScan.ApplySettingsAsync(settings) is invoked after the change
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable the hardware-button trigger on my .NET Android SparkScanView so warehouse workers can scan with a glove on. Use the volume-down key.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables hardware-trigger support via SparkScanViewSettings.HardwareTriggerEnabled = true and sets HardwareTriggerKeyCode to the volume-down key code, guarded by SparkScanView.HardwareTriggerSupported (which is only true on Android API 28+).

assertions.text: viewSettings.HardwareTriggerEnabled = true is set
viewSettings.HardwareTriggerKeyCode is assigned a volume-down key code (Android.Views.Keycode.VolumeDown cast to int, or a numeric literal in the range that represents KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN)
SparkScanView.HardwareTriggerSupported is checked (used as a static property guard) before enabling the trigger
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET Android SparkScan app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode via an async HTTP call, and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets sparkScan.Enabled = false at the start of the BarcodeScanned handler, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via RunOnUiThread.

assertions.text: sparkScan.Enabled = false is set inside the BarcodeScanned handler before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
sparkScan.Enabled = true is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
RunOnUiThread is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView, lifecycle) is preserved
